useCases.title: AI research report signoff
useCases.type: I delivered an AI research report to a client and need proof it is ready to accept.
useCases.goal: Turn a report into an acceptance-ready Mission Receipt.
steps: Paste the outcome you need accepted.
GoalOS defines done and lists the evidence required.
Reviewer accepts, requests changes, or rejects.
Receipt preserves what was delivered and what remains limited.

useCases.title: Client deliverable acceptance
useCases.type: A client needs to approve an AI-assisted automation project before final payment.
useCases.goal: Make acceptance explicit before reputation or payment changes.
steps: Describe the deliverable and what counts as done.
GoalOS maps work to acceptance gates.
Client reviews with a simple status.
Receipt becomes the shared record.

useCases.title: DAO grant milestone release
useCases.type: Release a DAO grant milestone only if the proof package passes review.
useCases.goal: Move from status update to proof-gated milestone readiness.
steps: State the grant milestone.
GoalOS identifies proof objects and reviewer authority.
DAO sees what passed, failed, or remains uncertain.
Funding decision is tied to evidence, not announcements.

useCases.title: Audit remediation proof
useCases.type: We fixed audit findings and need a replayable signoff package before release.
useCases.goal: Prove each fix is mapped to evidence and residual risk.
steps: Describe the findings and target release.
GoalOS maps every claim to proof.
Reviewer checks tests, replay, and rollback.
Release is marked ready only when gates pass.

useCases.title: Protocol upgrade readiness
useCases.type: Prepare a protocol upgrade for governance with proof, rollback, and reviewer authority.
useCases.goal: Turn an upgrade proposal into a governed decision package.
steps: Explain the proposed upgrade.
GoalOS identifies required evidence and stop conditions.
Reviewers inspect action, authority, and rollback.
Governance receives a clean decision object.

useCases.title: Enterprise AI workflow acceptance
useCases.audience: Enterprise buyer, AI delivery lead, compliance team
useCases.type: An internal AI automation is ready for business acceptance, but compliance needs evidence.
useCases.goal: Make AI work safe to accept inside an organization.
steps: Describe the workflow and prohibited claims.
GoalOS builds a review checklist.
Business owner decides accept or changes needed.
Receipt records scope and limits.

useCases.title: Understand the 48 Mainnet contracts
useCases.audience: Investor, auditor, protocol operator, technical reviewer
useCases.type: Show me which 48 Ethereum Mainnet contracts support Signoff Verified and Signoff & Settle.
useCases.goal: Make the protocol rails legible without forcing users into wallet operations.
steps: Ask for a contract family, tier, or stage.
GoalOS filters the rail map.
User opens the atlas or individual contract page.
Boundary stays read-only and educational.

useCases.title: Move-37 / RSI breakthrough governance
useCases.audience: Frontier lab, safety lead, Architect / Validator Council
useCases.type: Govern a Move-37 breakthrough candidate with replay, stress tests, and a dossier.
useCases.goal: Treat a breakthrough as an audited state transition, not a narrative.
steps: Describe the candidate and risk boundary.
GoalOS routes through deterministic RSI gates.
Candidate is reproduced, stress tested, and packaged.
Council receives a dossier rather than hype.

useCases.title: Independent reviewer package
useCases.type: I need an independent reviewer to decide whether the work deserves acceptance.
steps: Explain the work and reviewer role.
GoalOS lists what the reviewer must inspect.
Reviewer outcome is recorded.
Receipt states accepted, changes needed, or rejected.

useCases.title: Treasury payment readiness
useCases.type: Prepare payment release only after accepted proof and review.
useCases.goal: Keep payment separate from acceptance until evidence passes.
steps: State what value should move and why.
GoalOS first produces proof-to-acceptance.
Protocol rails are shown as optional later activation.
Payment readiness depends on proof, not persuasion.
